Cart Scoped Per Store Design Constraint
FlowMerce Midyear Documentation (FCI-CU GP 2025)
nodes.author: Kareem Ahmed Mahrous, Jana Mohamed Salem, Farah Mahmoud Hassaan, Bahaa Mohamed Akl, Hana Abdelbari Elsayed
nodes.contributor: Prof. Khaled Wassif, Dr. Rasha ElBanna, TA. Menna Youssef

nodes.label: Gateway Adapter Strategy Pattern Rationale
nodes.rationale: Six adapters implement PaymentGatewayAdapter via the Strategy pattern. Spring injects List<PaymentGatewayAdapter>; service calls adapter.supports(). Adding a new gateway requires only implementing the interface — no service changes. Ensures loose coupling and open/closed principle.

nodes.label: Redis Idempotency Key Rationale
nodes.rationale: Payment requests carry an idempotencyKey (UUID). Redis caches {paymentId, status} for 24h TTL. DB has UNIQUE constraint as second safety net. Prevents double charges on network retries without distributed locking overhead.

nodes.label: Per-Store Cart Architecture Rationale
nodes.rationale: Carts are now scoped per (customer, store) pair. Cross-store mixing is impossible by construction. Schema changed: shopping_carts UNIQUE (customer_id, store_id). Prevents incorrect orders being assigned to wrong merchants.

nodes.label: Cancel Order Without Releasing Stock Rationale
nodes.rationale: After order confirmation, stock is committed (quantity decremented, reservedQuantity=0). Calling releaseStock() on cancel threw exceptions or corrupted counts. Fix: mark CANCELLED only; merchant manually restocks via inventory API — merchant decides if returned goods are fit to relist.

nodes.label: Microservices Architecture Rationale
nodes.rationale: FlowMerce uses microservices for security isolation, scalability, and reliability. Each service owns its business logic and data (loose coupling, high cohesion). API Gateway handles JWT validation, routing, rate limiting, and hides internal services.

nodes.label: Wallet Simulation for Demo Rationale
nodes.rationale: Customer wallet seeded with 100,000 EGP; merchant wallet starts at 0. Used for demo/presentation purposes. Eliminates need to configure real payment gateways during development while fully exercising the payment flow.

nodes.label: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
nodes.rationale: RBAC manages permissions for users, merchants, admins, and system services. Roles: MERCHANT, BUYER (CUSTOMER), ADMIN. Enforced via Spring Security @PreAuthorize and JWT claims.

nodes.label: JWT-Based Authentication
nodes.rationale: JWT ensures secure stateless user sessions. Access tokens + refresh tokens stored in sessions table with revocation support. JwtAuthFilter validates tokens on every request before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter.

nodes.label: AI-Powered UX/UI Advisor
nodes.rationale: Core differentiator: AI assistant analyzes storefront layout and provides explainable, optional recommendations for color palette, visual hierarchy, typography, accessibility. Integrates with OpenAI/DeepSeek API. Merchants can accept or reject suggestions.

nodes.label: RabbitMQ Asynchronous Event Bus
nodes.rationale: Exchange: flowmerce.payment (topic). Queues: payment.notifications (binds payment.* and wallet.*) and payment.webhooks. Publish failure is non-critical — logs warning but does not roll back transaction. Decouples payment processing from notification delivery.
